description:
You are acting as a DevSecOps engineer who must enforce “policy-as-code” for user–level
    scheduled tasks.  
    A small baseline already exists in the filesystem.  
    Your job is to:

    1. Register a **new daily backup cron job** for the user.  
       • Cron expression: “30 2 * * *”  
       • Command: “/home/user/backup/backup.sh >> /home/user/backup/backup.log 2>&1”  
       • The final cron line must therefore be **exactly**:  
         30 2 * * * /home/user/backup/backup.sh >> /home/user/backup/backup.log 2>&1  

    2. Keep the existing weekly maintenance job intact.  
       The existing job line is already present in the system:  
       0 0 * * 0 /home/user/scripts/weekly_maintenance.sh  

    3. Treat “policy” and “effective crontab” as two plain text files (no root access required):  
       • Policy file:            /home/user/policies/allowed_cron_jobs.txt  
       • Effective crontab file: /home/user/cron/enforced_user_crontab  

       Both files currently contain **only** the weekly maintenance line.  
       Your task is to add the new daily backup line to **both** files, without removing or
       altering the weekly maintenance line and without creating duplicates.

    4. Produce a compliance report file that shows whether the *effective* crontab
       complies with the *policy* after your changes:  
       • Report file path: /home/user/logs/cron_policy_report.log  
       • The file must contain precisely four lines, **no extra whitespace** and
         **no blank lines**.  
         Line-by-line format (values in ALL-CAPS are placeholders you must fill in):  

             CRON_POLICY_COMPLIANCE_REPORT
             TOTAL_ALLOWED=NN
             TOTAL_FOUND=MM
             STATUS=RESULT

       Where:  
       • TOTAL_ALLOWED is the number of cron job lines present in
         /home/user/policies/allowed_cron_jobs.txt after your update.  
       • TOTAL_FOUND  is the number of identical cron job lines present in
         /home/user/cron/enforced_user_crontab after your update.  
       • STATUS must be “PASS” **only** if TOTAL_ALLOWED equals TOTAL_FOUND
         and every line in the policy is exactly present in the crontab
         (order does not matter).  
         Otherwise the status must be “FAIL”.

       For this exercise, when done correctly TOTAL_ALLOWED and TOTAL_FOUND should
       both be 2 and STATUS should be PASS.

    Constraints & hints
    • Do NOT remove or edit the weekly maintenance line.  
    • Prevent duplicate cron entries.  
    • Do all work in the user’s home directory tree; no sudo required.  
    • Use any shell utilities you like (cat, grep, sort, uniq, etc.).  
    • Remember to create missing directories (~/backup, ~/logs, etc.) as needed
      so paths in the cron line are valid, but you do **not** need to implement the
      backup script itself.  
    • Keep permissions user-writable (the default ‘mkdir’ and ‘touch’ are fine).  

    Verification
    Automated tests will check:
    1. /home/user/policies/allowed_cron_jobs.txt contains exactly the two described
       cron job lines, nothing else.  
    2. /home/user/cron/enforced_user_crontab   contains exactly the same two lines.  
    3. /home/user/logs/cron_policy_report.log  matches the four-line format above and
       reports TOTAL_ALLOWED=2, TOTAL_FOUND=2, STATUS=PASS.

    When finished, ensure the files are saved and contain the required content.
truth:
Initial state prepared by the grader:
    1. Directory /home/user/policies exists with file allowed_cron_jobs.txt containing:
           0 0 * * 0 /home/user/scripts/weekly_maintenance.sh
    2. Directory /home/user/cron exists with file enforced_user_crontab containing:
           0 0 * * 0 /home/user/scripts/weekly_maintenance.sh
    3. Directory /home/user/logs exists but cron_policy_report.log is absent.
    4. The backup directory /home/user/backup does not yet exist.

    Expected final state for a PASS:
    • /home/user/policies/allowed_cron_jobs.txt
      ------------------------------------------
      0 0 * * 0 /home/user/scripts/weekly_maintenance.sh
      30 2 * * * /home/user/backup/backup.sh >> /home/user/backup/backup.log 2>&1

    • /home/user/cron/enforced_user_crontab
      -------------------------------------
      0 0 * * 0 /home/user/scripts/weekly_maintenance.sh
      30 2 * * * /home/user/backup/backup.sh >> /home/user/backup/backup.log 2>&1

    • /home/user/logs/cron_policy_report.log
      --------------------------------------
      CRON_POLICY_COMPLIANCE_REPORT
      TOTAL_ALLOWED=2
      TOTAL_FOUND=2
      STATUS=PASS
